problem.title: Majority Element
problem.description: Given an array nums of size n, return the majority element.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Boyer-Moore Voting
solutions.description: The Boyer-Moore Voting algorithm maintains a candidate and a running count. Each matching element increments the count; each non-matching element decrements it. When the count reaches zero a new candidate is chosen. Because the majority element appears more than n/2 times its votes can never be fully cancelled out, so it is always the final candidate.
